title: Terence Tao uses ChatGPT to digest AI's Jacobian counterexample
summary: The Fields medalist shared his full ChatGPT session dissecting the AI-discovered counterexample that felled the 87-year-old Jacobian conjecture.
body:
Terence Tao, the UCLA mathematician and Fields medalist, has publicly shared the complete ChatGPT conversation he used while working through the newly discovered counterexample to the Jacobian conjecture. The transcript circulated widely on July 22, reaching the top of Hacker News, and gives an unusually candid view of how one of the world's leading mathematicians actually works alongside a language model — probing definitions, testing hunches, and asking the system to check computations.

## An 87-year-old conjecture falls

The Jacobian conjecture, posed by Ott-Heinrich Keller in 1939, asserts that any polynomial map from complex n-dimensional space to itself whose Jacobian determinant is a nonzero constant must be globally invertible. Last week the conjecture collapsed: a counterexample found with the help of Anthropic's Fable model, as Tao's post recounts, exhibits a degree-seven polynomial map on C^3 with constant Jacobian −2 that nevertheless sends three distinct points to the same output. The construction settles the question negatively for every dimension three and above, while the classical two-dimensional case remains open.

## From machine artifact to human understanding

In a July 21 blog post titled "A digestion of the Jacobian conjecture counterexample," Tao set out to make the machine-found object humanly comprehensible. Rather than repeating the raw formulas, he rebuilds the example geometrically from symmetric powers of linear and quadratic polynomials, reducing verification to three checkable properties: local injectivity, global non-injectivity, and affine equivalence to C^3. He states plainly that he used an AI chatbot to discuss aspects of the problem and to confirm several of the calculations — and then linked the session itself.

Why it matters: this is a live demonstration of a new division of labor in mathematics. An AI system produced a counterexample that surprised the field; a top human mathematician then used another AI system to translate that artifact into structural insight, and published the process transparently. The episode reframes the debate about AI in research — the question is no longer whether models can contribute to frontier mathematics, but how quickly working practices, verification norms, and credit conventions will adapt.
title_zh: 陶哲轩公开 ChatGPT 对话实录：拆解 AI 发现的雅可比猜想反例
summary_zh: 菲尔兹奖得主陶哲轩公开了自己与 ChatGPT 的完整对话，逐步消化 AI 找到的、推翻 87 年雅可比猜想的反例。
body_zh:
菲尔兹奖得主、UCLA 数学家陶哲轩公开了他研究雅可比猜想反例时与 ChatGPT 的完整对话记录。这份对话 7 月 22 日在社区广泛传播并登上 Hacker News 榜首，罕见地展示了一位顶尖数学家与大模型协作的真实过程——推敲定义、验证直觉、让模型复核计算。

## 87 年悬案被推翻

雅可比猜想由 Ott-Heinrich Keller 于 1939 年提出：若一个复 n 维空间到自身的多项式映射的雅可比行列式为非零常数，该映射必定全局可逆。上周这一猜想被推翻——据陶哲轩文中转述，反例是在 Anthropic 的 Fable 模型协助下找到的：一个 C^3 上的七次多项式映射，雅可比行列式恒为 −2，却把三个不同的点映到同一个输出。这一构造对三维及以上所有维度给出了否定回答，经典的二维情形仍然悬而未决。

## 从机器产物到人类理解

在 7 月 21 日题为《雅可比猜想反例的消化》的博客中，陶哲轩尝试把这个机器发现的对象变得可被人类理解。他没有照搬原始公式，而是用线性与二次多项式的对称幂从几何上重建整个反例，把验证工作拆解为三条可逐一核对的性质：局部单射、整体非单射、以及与 C^3 的仿射等价。他明确写道，自己借助 AI 聊天机器人讨论了问题的多个侧面并核实了若干计算，随后干脆把对话原文一并公开。

为什么重要：这是数学研究新分工的一次现场演示。AI 系统先找到了让整个领域意外的反例，顶尖人类数学家再借助另一个 AI 系统把这个产物翻译成结构性洞见，并全程透明公开。关于 AI 能否参与前沿数学的争论已经翻篇——真正的问题是，研究习惯、验证规范和署名惯例要多快才能跟上。

sources.title: A digestion of the Jacobian conjecture counterexample — Terence Tao

featured_reason: A Fields medalist openly working with AI to digest an AI-discovered disproof of an 87-year-old conjecture is the clearest milestone yet for machine-assisted mathematics.
featured_reason_zh: 菲尔兹奖得主公开借助 AI 消化 AI 推翻 87 年猜想的反例，是机器辅助数学迄今最清晰的里程碑。
